title: Jill Yates, K.C.
content:
Managing Partner, BC Region
People.ContactEmail jyates@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2002
- University of British Columbia
Recognized for her strategic judgment and exceptional courtroom skill, Jill helps clients navigate and resolve complex commercial and class action disputes, with a proven ability to win high‑stakes cases that shape legal and market outcomes.
Jill Yates is our Regional Managing Partner for British Columbia, Chair of the National Class Actions Group, and a partner in our Litigation Group in Vancouver. Appointed King’s Counsel (KC) in 2026, Jill is recognized for excellence in the practice of law and a demonstrated commitment to community service. She blends strategic judgment, practical problem-solving, and deep procedural experience to guide clients through complex class actions, product liability, financial services and commercial litigation matters.
Clients value Jill’s ability to simplify complicated legal problems, identify priorities, develop a strategy to navigate complex challenges, and arrive at a winning resolution. A seasoned trial lawyer, Jill leads the defence of high-stakes legal disputes for major international and Canadian companies operating in diverse industries, including financial services, retail and consumer markets, technology, pharmaceuticals, forestry, and manufacturing. She has led significant litigation mandates for major Canadian and global businesses, including The Toronto-Dominion Bank, Kimberly-Clark, CN, Cargill, Syngenta, and PepsiCo, among others.
Jill is a highly effective litigator widely recognized by Chambers , The Legal 500 , Lexpert , Who’s Who Legal , Best Lawyers , and Benchmark Litigation , and is consistently praised for her clarity, strategic insight, collaborative approach, and ability to deliver outstanding outcomes.
As Chair of the National Class Actions Group, Jill is a proven leader in the defence of major class action proceedings involving consumer protection, privacy, price‑fixing, drip‑pricing, product liability, financial products and services, discrimination and public law issues, regulatory matters, and more.
She has a record of success at all stages of the class action lifecycle — pre‑certification, certification, appeals, and trial — including securing a full trial victory for a major financial institution after a rare nine‑week common issues trial in a multi‑billion‑dollar claim.
Jill acts as lead counsel in complex commercial litigation, driving efficient and favourable resolutions for clients facing significant business, operational, or reputational challenges.
She also regularly represents financial institutions in litigation involving significant financial, regulatory, and reputational risk, including both class actions and complex commercial disputes.
Select experience includes:
- representing major real estate owners and developers in litigation and arbitration involving land sales, development conditions, leasing, and municipal issues
- obtaining and resisting urgent injunctions across sectors
- successfully representing a landowner and resource extraction company in a major conspiracy and fraud dispute through a nine‑week trial and subsequent appeal, securing complete success and a substantial costs award.
Jill is a contributing author to the sixth edition of Defending Class Actions in Canada (LexisNexis), and has contributed to previous editions. She is a frequent presenter on class actions and other litigation topics, an active participant in the legal and business communities in Vancouver, and  is recognized  among Business in Vancouver ’s BC 500 as one of the province’s most influential business leaders.
Deeply committed to community impact, Jill serves as Chair of BGC (formerly Boys and Girls Club) of South Coast B.C. and co-chairs McCarthy Tétrault’s Vancouver United Way Committee.
Jill has won various academic medals and awards, including the Gold Medal and the Governor General’s Medal in 1998.
